name: Shared Git Utils
description: Shared git helpers consumed by the VS Code extension. Hand-written ESM+CJS package (no build step), following the same dual-format convention as @code-workbench/mcp-core. Currently exposes the robust `git worktree remove` core that handles Windows lock failures, timeouts, and the "still-locked → detach + queue for later" rescue path.
guidelines: Caller injects `runGit` (already bound to the repo cwd) so the same execution path / WSL handling / timeout semantics as the rest of their codebase apply.
Caller owns pre-cleanup (close watchers, kill terminals inside the worktree) and post-cleanup (cache invalidation, UI refresh). This module only owns the git+fs dance.
PendingDeletionStore is an injected interface — the extension uses VS Code globalState. It rescues locked Windows directories.
Set detachOnLock=false for distro/WSL worktrees whose `.git` pointer isn't readable from the host filesystem.
Maintain ESM (.mjs) and CJS (.cjs) source manually in lockstep — keep them in semantic sync (matches mcp-core convention; no build step).
anti_patterns: Don't put VS Code imports here — this package must stay runtime-agnostic.
Don't add a tsc build step just for this package; keep the hand-maintained dual-format convention used by mcp-core.
Don't swallow non-lock errors — only EBUSY/EPERM/ENOTEMPTY/'Permission denied'/'failed to delete'/timeout/'is not a working tree' fall through to the manual-rm path; anything else must propagate.
decisions: Extracted into a shared package so the VS Code extension uses the robust removal path instead of a naive `git worktree remove --force; fs.rm` that lacked timeouts, retries, detach-on-lock, and pending-deletion recovery.
Dependency injection (runGit + pendingDeletions) chosen over importing concrete git runners — keeps the package free of VS Code coupling and lets the consumer keep its existing gitRaw quirks.

Reload-dance for deleting the currently-open worktree stays in the VS Code extension layer (vscode-specific concern); after reload, the resume path uses this shared core so retry/detach/pending-deletion gains apply there too.
